sentence_reviews.text: A self-organizing map uses competitive learning to place prototype vectors on a low-dimensional lattice that approximately preserves neighborhood relationships.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Deep Learning/PML unsupervised representation and prototype/clustering background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The definition is standard for SOMs, but the packet lacks a dedicated Kohonen/SOM source, so direct support is limited.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Self-organizing maps & competitive learning is to connect Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA/PML unsupervised learning and representation topics.
sentence_reviews.notes: Unsupervised and representation connections are supported; tabular decision-support downstream role is not directly established.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
sentence_reviews.notes: The packet sources do not directly support SOMs as a substrate for this downstream application.
SOMs are normally unsupervised/competitive learning methods; direct SOM-specific citation is absent.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA/PML unsupervised learning and representation background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dependencies are plausible; broad limitation wording is generic and not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: SOM/competitive learning is normally unsupervised; exact SOM evidence is not in the packet source set.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Prototype vectors are representations, but 'embeddings & distributed representations' is broader than directly supported.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity.
sentence_reviews.notes: Competitive learning has local-update flavor, but the cited sources do not directly connect SOMs to the full local plasticity label.

sentence_reviews.text: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Geometry, topology & symmetry.
sentence_reviews.notes: Neighborhood preservation/topographic maps make geometry/topology plausible, but direct evidence is weak.

edge_reviews.notes: trained_under/core is appropriate for SOM/competitive learning.
represents_with/core is plausible via prototype vectors/topographic representation, but direct packet support is limited.
trained_under/common is plausible for local competitive updates, but the dependency label is broader than the evidence.
requires/common is plausible from neighborhood-preserving maps, but direct support is weak.
